site_title: 주간 AI ｜ AI 활용 기계공학 논문 브리핑

ai.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
ai.summary: Agent skills today are hand-crafted, generated one-shot, or evolved through loosely controlled self-revision, none of which behaves like a deep-learning optimizer for the skill, and none of which reliably improves over its starting point under feedback. We argue the skill should instead be trained as the external state of a frozen agent, with the same discipline that makes weight-space optimization reproducible. SkillOpt is, to our knowledge, the first systematic controllable text-space optimizer for agent skills: a separate optimizer model turns scored rollouts into bounded add/delete/replace edits on a single skill document, and an edit is accepted only when it strictly improves a held-out validation score. A textual learning-rate budget, rejected-edit buffer, and epoch-wise slow/meta update make skill training stable while adding zero inference-time model calls at deployment. Across six benchmarks, seven target models, and three execution harnesses (direct chat, Codex, Claude Code), SkillOpt is best or tied on all 52 evaluated (model, benchmark, harness) cells and beats every per-cell competitor among human, one-shot LLM, Trace2Skill, TextGrad, GEPA, and EvoSkill skills. On GPT-5.5 it lifts the average no-skill accuracy by +23.5 points in direct chat, by +24.8 inside the Codex agentic loop, and by +19.1 inside Claude Code. Transfer experiments further show that optimized skill artifacts retain value when moved across model scales, between Codex and Claude Code execution environments, and to a nearby math benchmark without further optimization.
ai.title_ko: SkillOpt: 스스로 진화하는 에이전트 기술을 위한 실행 전략
ai.summary_ko:
- 기존 에이전트 기술은 수작업, 일회성 생성, 또는 느슨한 자가 수정으로 개발되어 신뢰성 있는 개선이 어려웠습니다.
- SkillOpt는 에이전트 기술을 고정된 에이전트의 외부 상태로 훈련하는 체계적인 텍스트 공간 최적화 방법입니다.
- 별도의 최적화 모델이 점수화된 실행 결과를 바탕으로 기술 문서에 추가/삭제/교체 편집을 제안하고, 유효성 검사 점수가 향상될 때만 적용합니다.
- SkillOpt는 6개 벤치마크, 7개 모델, 3개 실행 환경에서 모든 52개 평가 항목에서 최고 성능을 달성했으며, GPT-5.5에서 평균 정확도를 19.1~24.8점 향상시켰습니다.
- 이 방법은 배포 시 추가 추론 호출 없이 안정적인 기술 훈련을 가능하게 하며, 최적화된 기술의 전이성도 입증했습니다.

ai.title: TransitLM: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Map-Free Transit Route Generation
ai.summary: Public transit route planning traditionally depends on structured map infrastructure and complex routing engines, and no existing dataset supports training models to bypass this dependency. We present TransitLM, a large-scale dataset of over 13 million transit route planning records from four Chinese cities covering 120,845 stations and 13,666 lines, released as a continual pre-training corpus and benchmark data for three evaluation tasks with complementary metrics. Experiments show that an LLM trained on TransitLM produces structurally valid routes at high accuracy and implicitly grounds arbitrary GPS coordinates to appropriate stations without any explicit mapping. These results demonstrate that transit route planning can be learned entirely from data, enabling end-to-end, map-free route generation directly from origin-destination information. The dataset and benchmark are available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/GD-ML/TransitLM, with evaluation code at https://github.com/HotTricker/TransitLM.
ai.title_ko: TransitLM: 지도 없이 대중교통 경로를 생성하기 위한 대규모 데이터셋 및 벤치마크
ai.summary_ko:
- 기존 대중교통 경로 계획은 지도 인프라와 복잡한 라우팅 엔진에 의존하며, 이를 우회하는 모델 학습용 데이터셋은 없었습니다.
- 본 연구는 4개 중국 도시의 1,300만 개 이상 대중교통 경로 기록을 담은 대규모 데이터셋 TransitLM을 제안합니다.
- TransitLM은 12만 845개 역과 1만 3,666개 노선을 포함하며, 연속 사전 학습 코퍼스 및 벤치마크로 활용됩니다.
- 실험 결과, TransitLM으로 학습된 LLM은 높은 정확도로 유효한 경로를 생성하고 GPS 좌표를 역에 암묵적으로 연결합니다.
- 이는 대중교통 경로 계획이 데이터만으로 학습 가능하며, 출발지-목적지 정보로부터 종단간(end-to-end) 지도 없는 경로 생성을 가능하게 함을 보여줍니다.

mech.title: RL-100: Performant Robotic Manipulation with Real-World Reinforcement Learning
mech.summary: Real-world robotic manipulation in homes and factories demands reliability, efficiency, and robustness that approach or surpass those of skilled human operators. We present RL-100, a real-world reinforcement learning framework built on diffusion visuomotor policies. RL-100 unifies imitation and reinforcement learning under a single clipped PPO surrogate objective applied within the denoising process, yielding conservative and stable improvements across offline and online stages. To meet deployment latency requirements, a lightweight consistency distillation method compresses multi-step diffusion into a one-step controller for high-frequency control. The framework is task-, embodiment-, and representation-agnostic, and supports both single-action and action-chunking control. We evaluate RL-100 on eight diverse real-robot tasks, from dynamic pushing and agile bowling to pouring, cloth folding, unscrewing, multi-stage juicing, and long-horizon box folding. RL-100 attains 100 percent success across evaluated trials, for a total of 1000 out of 1000 episodes, including up to 250 out of 250 consecutive trials on one task. It matches or surpasses expert teleoperators in time to completion. Without retraining, a single policy attains approximately 90 percent zero-shot success under environmental and dynamics shifts, adapts in a few-shot regime to significant task variations (86.7 percent), and remains robust to aggressive human perturbations (about 96 percent). Notably, our juicing robot served random customers continuously for about seven hours without failure when deployed zero-shot in a shopping mall. These results suggest a practical path to deployment-ready robot learning by starting from human priors, aligning training objectives with human-grounded metrics, and reliably extending performance beyond human demonstrations.
mech.title_ko: RL-100: 실제 환경 강화 학습을 통한 고성능 로봇 조작
mech.summary_ko:
- 가정 및 공장 환경에서 로봇 조작은 인간 작업자 수준의 신뢰성과 효율성이 요구됩니다.
- RL-100은 모방 학습과 강화 학습을 통합한 확산 시각운동 정책 기반 프레임워크입니다.
- 이 프레임워크는 PPO(Proximal Policy Optimization) 목표를 활용하여 안정적인 성능 개선을 이룹니다.
- RL-100은 8가지 실제 로봇 작업에서 100% 성공률을 달성했으며, 총 1000회 중 1000회 성공했습니다.
- 사람의 사전 지식을 활용하여 실제 배포 가능한 로봇 학습의 실용적인 길을 제시합니다.

mech.title: Parameter Estimation of Power Electronic Converter by Affine Physics-Informed Gaussian Processes
mech.summary: Noninvasive extraction of converter parameters under specific operational conditions, often referred to as digital twinning, enables detailed insights into power electronics devices under real conditions. In this work, power electronics parameter estimation is addressed by proposing the construction of affine operators applied to physics-informed Gaussian processes. In particular, we show that the proposed parameter estimation technique has the following four key features: first, any converter parameter of interest can be estimated. second, noisy measurements are explicitly addressed. third, the converter parameters are characterized subject to the specific operational regime. four, The estimation accuracy is within the error margin of experimental characterization. Regarding model-building aspects, we define a dedicated noise-handling scheme that allows outperforming alternative algorithms when applied to hardware tests. We analyze the numerical stability of the presented approach and define a self-consistent normalization scheme allowing a reliable and numerically stable application. The proposed approach is theoretically discussed, analyzed within a simulation environment, and verified experimentally. For the latter, we investigate a hardware test addressing the two most prominent converter topologies, i.e., buck and boost topologies. Converter parameters are accurately estimated with an accuracy matched by experimental characterization error margins.
